name: when-debugging-ml-training-use-ml-training-debugger
description: Debug ML training issues and optimize performance including loss divergence, overfitting, and slow convergence
capabilities: Diagnose training issues including loss divergence, NaN values, overfitting, and slow convergence
Analyze root causes through gradient analysis, data checks, and architecture review
Apply fixes such as learning rate adjustment, dropout, L2 regularization, and gradient clipping
Validate fixes with before/after comparison, retraining, and visualization
Generate optimization recommendations for further improvement
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing example Python code snippets for ML training debugging. No executable scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or external commands. Pure educational content matching stated purpose. All 47 static findings are false positives.
valueStatement: ML training can fail silently with issues like loss divergence, NaN values, overfitting, and gradient problems. This skill provides a systematic 5-phase workflow to diagnose root causes, apply fixes, and validate improvements with proven techniques.
userTitle: Debug ML training issues and optimize performance
limitations: Requires training history data in JSON format with loss and accuracy metrics
Designed for TensorFlow and PyTorch models only
Does not handle distributed training or multi-GPU scenarios
Assumes access to compute resources for model retraining

useCases.title: Fix broken training runs
useCases.description: Diagnose why training loss exploded to NaN or stopped converging unexpectedly

useCases.title: Reduce overfitting
useCases.description: Identify and fix models that perform well on training data but poorly on validation

useCases.title: Optimize model performance
useCases.description: Systematically improve training stability and convergence speed for deep learning projects

promptTemplates.title: Quick diagnosis
promptTemplates.prompt: My model training loss is NaN after epoch 10. Use ml-training-debugger to diagnose and fix the issue.

promptTemplates.title: Overfitting fix
promptTemplates.prompt: My model has 95 percent training accuracy but only 60 percent validation accuracy. Use ml-training-debugger to identify and fix the overfitting.

promptTemplates.title: Slow convergence
promptTemplates.prompt: My training loss has not changed in 50 epochs. Use ml-training-debugger to analyze convergence rate and recommend fixes.

promptTemplates.title: Complete debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: Debug my training run with training_history.json. Diagnose all issues, apply fixes, and validate improvement with before and after comparison.

bestPractices: Save training history at every epoch to enable post-hoc diagnosis of issues
Monitor gradients during training to catch vanishing or exploding issues early
Always validate fixes with a complete retraining run, not just a few iterations
Use learning rate schedulers and early stopping callbacks as standard practice
antiPatterns: Training without saving history or monitoring validation metrics
Ignoring validation loss while tracking only training loss progress
Making multiple changes simultaneously without isolating variables to test
Training on insufficient data and expecting good generalization results

faq.question: Which ML frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: TensorFlow Keras and PyTorch are fully supported. The diagnostic patterns apply broadly to most deep learning frameworks.

faq.question: What training history format is required?
faq.answer: The skill expects a JSON file with loss, val_loss, accuracy, and val_accuracy arrays for each training epoch.

faq.question: Can this skill fix gradient explosion in RNNs?
faq.answer: Yes. The gradient analysis phase detects exploding gradients and applies gradient clipping with clipnorm 1.0 as a fix.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my model files?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates a fixed_model.h5 file with corrected architecture. Your original model file remains unchanged.

faq.question: What if multiple issues are detected?
faq.answer: The skill prioritizes issues by severity from critical to high to medium to low and applies fixes in that order systematically.

faq.question: How does this compare to TensorBoard debugging?
faq.answer: This skill provides automated diagnosis and code level fixes. TensorBoard provides visualization. Use both together for best results.
